* feat: org, inventory, and actionLog schemas with backend functions

- org + orgMember tables with leader/officer/member roles
- inventory table replacing craftingInventory (supports all item types)
- general actionLog table for all scoped actions
- org CRUD: create, invite, remove, updateRole, listMembers
- inventory CRUD: upsertOre, upsertItem, removeItem, clearByKind

* feat: inventory UI, game items data, config restructure

- Player inventory page with Add Item dialog (search 2795 game items)
- Category filter chips, description previews, quality for materials only
- Game items extracted from SC Data.p4k entity records via Datarunner
- Config restructured: data/items/, data/mining/, data/crafting/, data/starmap/
- Removed duplicate/dead config files (bp.json, blueprints-all.json, etc.)
- qualityToTier helper, useInventory hook, PageInfoDialog on inventory

* feat: blueprint collection page with filters, sign-in banner

- Player blueprints shows all BPs with owned on top, checkmark toggle
- ToggleGroup filters: All/Owned/Missing + Weapons/Armour/Ammo
- Search by name, category, or material
- Removed owned column from crafting/blueprints (moved to player page)
- Sign-in banner on crafting/blueprints for unauthenticated users

* Add AppShell usePageHeader and internal docs

Introduce AppShell with a PageHeader context and usePageHeader hook.
Update tools layout and pages to use the shell and wire headers.
Add internal CIG data mining policy and a comprehensive game.log
reference.

* feat: login page, auth banner, and dashboard

* feat: custom error, global-error and not-found pages

* Remove: craftingInventory and update inventory

Remove legacy craftingInventory table, server resolvers, and migration
and delete the frontend useCraftingInventory hook. Update the inventory
schema and DB indexes to include kind in name lookups and adjust backend
upsert queries to use the new index and include blueprintId. Also fix
useInventory isLoading handling and refactor usePageHeader effect
dependencies to satisfy exhaustive deps rules.

* fix: constrain orgMember role to valid literals, fix blueprint collection isLoading

feat: voxel robot models + art style doc

Replace Kenney speeder GLBs with a procedural BoxMesh droid built entirely
in GDScript — no external asset downloads required.

Shape (all BoxMesh, matte roughness=0.95 metallic=0.05):
  body      0.50x0.30x0.38 — full player color
  head      0.30x0.24x0.28 — 82% player color (slightly darker)
  tracks x2 0.10x0.10x0.44 — 55% player color (dark shadow)
  antenna   0.04x0.15x0.04 — 65% player color
  eyes x2   0.07x0.05x0.03 — emissive cyan glow (not tinted)

Implementation notes:
- Parts use neutral grey on BoxMesh.material so _tint_meshes multiplies
  cleanly (grey * player_color = correct shade per part)
- _eyes_root is a sibling of _model_root so _tint_meshes ignores eyes
- mark_dead() dims eyes + disables emission; revive() restores them
- _tint_meshes now preserves source roughness/metallic instead of
  hard-coding 0.45/0.30 (was a leftover from the speeder GLB tinting)
- LABEL_Y raised 0.75->0.90, HP_BAR_Y raised 1.05->1.15 to clear antenna

Also adds docs/ART_STYLE.md covering the full 5-phase voxel style plan.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/marcelomatus/gtopt/sessions/0279f351-a218-4e44-9a43-f858eac0927b

* fix: make scripts-install-deps skip gracefully instead of failing

When pip install fails (missing system deps, network issues, etc.), the
scripts-install-deps CTest fixture now returns exit code 77 instead of
failing with FATAL_ERROR.  Combined with SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77, CTest
marks the fixture as *skipped* and all 27 dependent script tests are
also properly skipped (CMP0175 NEW, cmake >= 3.31) rather than being
counted as failures.

Changes:
- New scripts/cmake/install_deps_helper.py: Python script that returns
  0 (success) or 77 (skip) instead of the old cmake -P script that
  could only return 0 or 1 (FATAL_ERROR).
- Updated scripts/CMakeLists.txt: use the Python helper and add
  SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77 to the fixture test properties.

commit.message:
Add ant_train: full Rust/Candle standalone training binary

Complete Rust implementation of ANT (937K params) using Candle:
- ant_train/src/model.rs: RMSNorm, RoPE, AddrNet, MemoryAttention, TransformerBlock, ANT
- ant_train/src/trie.rs: Self-contained HierarchicalTrie (no PyO3 dep)
- ant_train/src/engine.rs: ANTEngine with per-token READ→PROCESS→WRITE cycle
- ant_train/src/train.rs: Phase A/B/C curriculum, AdamW, safetensors checkpoints
- ant_train/src/data.rs: Data loading from data_cache/, byte tokenization
- ant_train/src/main.rs: CLI entry (--device, --steps-a/b/c, --lr-a/b/c, etc.)
- ant_train/src/inference.rs: Interactive chat via generate()
- Features: cuda, metal

Smoke tested: all 3 phases run end-to-end, loss decreasing, checkpoints save.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: poll scraper task endpoint instead of treating running as failure

The scraper addon uses fire-and-poll: POST /api/discover returns
{task_id, status: running} immediately, then the client must poll
GET /api/task/{id} until status is done. The frontend was treating
the initial running response as a failure, showing 'Product not
found online' before the lookup even completed.

Now polls every 2s for up to 120s. Bump to v1.14.3.

scans for stub products (created by the recipe scraper with description

a real parent product. Recipe positions are moved from the stub to the

the recipe still references an orphaned stub 'suola'.

commit.message:
chore: Fix OSS upload path nesting and missing checkout in release oss_backup job (#89)

`oss_backup` in `release.yml` uploaded files under double-nested paths
(e.g.
`.../v0.15.0/longbridge-terminal-linux-musl-amd64.tar.gz/longbridge-terminal-linux-musl-amd64.tar.gz`)
because `download-artifact` creates one subdirectory per artifact by
default. Additionally, the job had no `checkout`, so
`.scoop/longbridge.json` was never present and the scoop manifest upload
silently failed.

## `release.yml` — `oss_backup` job

- **`merge-multiple: true`** on `download-artifact@v4` — flattens all
artifacts directly into `release-dist/` instead of per-artifact subdirs
- **Add `actions/checkout@v4`** — makes `.scoop/longbridge.json`
available for the manifest step
- **Sanity-check step** — fails fast if any subdirectories remain in
`release-dist/` after download (regression guard)
- **`set -euo pipefail`** on all bash steps + replace `cat file | sed`
with `sed ... file` to ensure pipe failures propagate

## `test-oss-backup.yml` → `release-to-oss.yml` (manual fallback)

- Renamed and updated workflow name to `Release to OSS`
- Added optional `tag` input: uses provided tag or falls back to latest
release
- Same bash hardening applied
- Added `permissions: contents: read` to scope `GITHUB_TOKEN`

* daprds: Adds SIGHUP handler reloader

Adds support to daprd to handle SIGHUP posix signals which will restart
the daprd runtime in process. This is useful for reloading Configuration
etc.

Linking this functionality with the HotReloading feature will be added
in a follow-up PR.

Signed-off-by: joshvanl <me@joshvanl.dev>

* hotreload: Configuration, HTTPEndpoint, and Resiliency

Adds ConfigurationUpdate and ResiliencyUpdate gRPC streaming RPCs to the
operator API (matching the existing ComponentUpdate/HTTPEndpointUpdate
pattern), and introduces a SIGHUPReconciler that watches these streams
and triggers a runtime restart only when a resource has actually changed.

The reconciler uses the compstore to look up existing resources rather
than maintaining a separate known map, so unchanged update events are
detected via `differ.AreSame` and silently ignored.

- Add ConfigurationUpdate and ResiliencyUpdate RPCs to operator proto
- Add Configuration and Resiliency types to the differ.Resource interface
  (Kind, APIVersion, EmptyMetaDeepCopy, etc.)
- Add compstore storage for raw configapi.Configuration and
  resiliencyapi.Resiliency API objects
- Store initial config and resiliency resources in compstore at startup
- Return raw configapi.Configuration from LoadKubernetesConfiguration
- Add operator loader and store wrappers for all three resource types
- Add integration tests for all three operator update streams
- Add SIGHUP integration tests for change and no-change scenarios

* hotreload: read disk resource files once per fsnotify event

When an fsnotify event fires in self-hosted mode, each resource type
(components, subscriptions, configurations, httpendpoints, resiliencies)
independently opens and reads every YAML file in the resource
directories. With 5 resource types, this means 5 full directory scans
per event. On Windows, this creates a race where os.Remove() in tests
(or external processes) collides with os.Open() in the loader, causing
file deletion to fail because Windows doesn't allow deleting files with
open handles.

Introduce a dirdata subpackage that reads all YAML files into memory in
a single pass. The pre-read data is then passed to each resource type
for parsing, reducing file open/close cycles from 5x to 1x per event.

commit.message:
Add pause/resume reading controls

While reading aloud, the user can now:
- Tap Pause (amber) to freeze the assessment mid-session
- Tap Resume (green) to continue from where they left off
- Tap Stop (red) at any time to end and see scores

On resume, the recognizer restarts from the exact word group
the user left off at (trimming already-assessed words from a
partially-completed sentence). The MediaRecorder is also
paused/resumed to keep the audio recording continuous.
